enter: verb. To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
entirety: noun. The whole; the complete or amount.
entity: noun. That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit, often used for organizations which have no physical form.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyer: noun. One who eyes someone or something.
eyne: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
eyre: noun. A journey taken by certain Medieval English itinerant judges (justices in eyre)
eyrie: noun. The nest of a bird of prey.
inter: verb. To bury in a grave.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
netter: noun. One who nets (in any sense), or who uses a net.
nettier: adjective. comparative form of netty: more netty
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
niter: noun. A mineral form of potassium nitrate (saltpetre) used in making gunpowder.
nitery: noun. Alternative form of niterie (“night club”).
nitre: noun. UK standard spelling of niter.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rent: noun. A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to lease a property.
rente: noun. In France, interest payable by government on indebtedness; the bonds, shares, stocks, etc. that represent government indebtedness.
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rete: noun. A network of blood vessels or nerves.
retie: verb. To tie again; to tie something that has already been tied or was tied before.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tenet: noun. An opinion, belief, or principle that is held as absolute truth by someone or especially an organization.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
tire: verb. To become sleepy or weary.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titer: noun. The concentration of a substance as determined by titration.
titre: noun. The strength or concentration of a solution that has been determined by titration.
tree: noun. A perennial woody plant taller and larger than a shrub with a wooden trunk and, at some distance from the ground, having leaves and
tret: noun. An allowance to purchasers, for waste or refuse matter, of four pounds on every 104 pounds of suttle weight, or weight after the tare
trey: noun. A playing card or die with the rank of three.
triene: noun. Any alkene that has three double bonds
trite: adjective. used so many times that it is commonplace, or no longer interesting or effective; worn out, hackneyed.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
tye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kyenga.
tyee: noun. A tribal chief of the Chinookan peoples in the Pacific Northwest of the United States.
tyer: noun. Dated form of tier (“a person or device that ties”).
tyre: noun. The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or
yen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Yendang.
yet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yetfa.
yeti: noun. An unidentified humanoid animal said to live in the Himalayas.
yin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yinchia.
